evidence_quotes.quote: the shutdown and/or destruction of digital products and services in video games.

paragraphs.text: As an EU citizen and consumer, I welcome efforts to strengthen digital fairness. However, I wish to highlight a growing yet overlooked issue: the shutdown and/or destruction of digital products and services in video games. This often renders products that were legally bought and paid for completely unusable without any options for consumers to continue using the product or service, even in a perhaps limited state. Recently especially video games have increasingly often become unplayable when publishers take servers offline or otherwise discontinue support. Many titles rely heavily on online components, and those with DRM or alwaysonline requirements are especially affected, even if they include singleplayer modes. This trend leaves consumers without access to products they paid for and undermines trust. To address this, publishers should at minimum: 1. disclose server shutdown timelines at purchase, 2. allow communityhosted servers, and 3. avoid DRM/alwaysonline restrictions for primarily singleplayer games. Current practices amount to planned obsolescence, data loss, and manipulative design not currently covered by any form of consumer protection. In my opinion they clearly fall under the scope of the EUs digital fairness goals. I urge you to include this issue in the legislation.
